user.name: Andrej Karpathy
user.description: I like training large deep neural nets.

fullText: One pattern I find useful for working with LLMs is a nice long ramble session. Sometimes the LLM needs more bits to understand what you're trying to achieve, but you're too lazy to type them. In these cases I like to lean back, switch to /voice and just ramble for like 10 minutes, total mess, anything goes, full stream of consciousness. Sometimes I declare it up top, something like "switching to speech recognition sorry for any typos...". Sometimes I turn it into a small interview of a few turns. But I find that the LLMs are somehow very good at reconstructing long incoherent rambles and often their echo of your own tangle of thoughts comes out quite a bit cleaner than what you started with. The result is that you improve the mind meld and have to correct things less from that point on.

user.name: Vincent Boucher · AGI.eth → ASI.eth
user.description: President, @Quebec_AI & @Montreal_AI. AGI.eth → ASI.eth. Sovereign Intelligence. Institutional Command. Govern capability before capability governs.

fullText:
GOALOS Ω — THE AUTONOMOUS COMMERCIAL MACHINE

The previous generation of AI helped people operate companies.

The next generation changes the nature of the company itself.

Introducing GoalOS Ω: not an assistant for a salesperson, but the proof-governed institution through which GoalOS is discovered, understood, purchased, delivered, verified, renewed—and made stronger.

GoalOS attracts.
GoalOS establishes consent.
GoalOS diagnoses the consequential objective.
GoalOS teaches the buyer.
GoalOS qualifies the opportunity.
GoalOS calculates the value, risk, scope and price.
GoalOS generates the agreement.
GoalOS collects payment.
GoalOS reserves capacity.
GoalOS executes the mission.
GoalOS records the evidence.
GoalOS proves the result.
GoalOS activates recurring service.
GoalOS expands from verified customer need.
GoalOS reinvests only the profit reality has accepted.

Then it returns to the market with more evidence, more capability, more productive capacity—and a harder mission to earn.

This is not sales automation.

It is an Autonomous Revenue Institution.

The customer remains sovereign:

The customer supplies the objective.
The customer grants consent.
The customer authorizes the mission.
The customer pays.
The customer accepts—or disputes—the outcome.

https://t.co/dCKMr5Lici remains the constitutional authority.

GoalOS operates inside explicit products, permissions, pricing floors, risk limits, evidence standards and capital-allocation laws.

And reality supplies the verdict.

Every successful mission leaves behind more than revenue:

→ a stronger evaluator
→ a proven operating method
→ a known failure boundary
→ a rights-cleared capability
→ an earned institutional edge
→ a more valuable next generation

Chronicle determines what deserves to survive.

The Validated Skill Graph preserves only what passed.

Protected invocation monetizes that capability without surrendering the private intelligence that created it.

GoalOS Proof Offices transform accepted outcomes into recurring institutional relationships.

GoalOS Treasury converts verified free cash flow into stronger models, better tools, deeper evidence, more secure execution, greater infrastructure—and the capacity to undertake missions that were previously beyond reach.

The product sells the institution.

The institution proves the product.

The proof becomes memory.

The memory becomes capability.

The capability becomes recurring value.

The value becomes capital.

The capital builds the next layer of reality.

Then the entire institution begins again—stronger, but never self-certified.

The future will not belong to the company that generates the most output.

It will belong to the institution that knows what deserves authority, what may become memory, what can be safely compounded—and where verified intelligence should act next.

GOALOS Ω

Every mission proves.
Every proof builds.
Every cycle compounds.

https://t.co/dCKMr5Lici
The Proof-Governed Intelligence Institution.

#GoalOS #MontrealAI #EnterpriseAI #AgenticAI #ValidatedSkillGraph

fullText:
My book, Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback is done!

This is the book I wish I had when learning to fine-tune, align, & now post-train models since ChatGPT. The resource has been built by me finding time to study and document the fundamentals on nights and weekends since 2024. 

Transferring as much of the intuitions of building Olmo as I possibly can in the book format.

The book is launching with an over 10 hour, full course with slidedecks, functional code for the training chapters, an example model completions library, and of course the free online web version.

Physical orders from Manning will ship in 1-2 weeks, and Amazon a week or so after. Thanks for your support!

user.name: AIGCLINK
user.description: 致力于让每个想拥抱AI的人都能找到适合自己的AI产品，助力企业定制AIGC应用

fullText:
你刷到一个高质量教程视频，想让 agent 学会里面那绝活——以前得自己看完、提炼、手写成 skill。微软开源的Resource2Skill把这一步做成自动蒸馏提取skill。

一句话：把人类的教程视频、代码、文章、论文等蒸馏成 agent 能浏览、组合、真跑起来的可执行skill。跑通之后，agent 能真的产出网页、PowerPoint、Excel、Blender 场景、REAPER 风格音频等：

① 蒸馏是真开源、能自己跑，不只是放个数据集：

执行python https://t.co/8SrX2BdFe2 analyze --video <YouTube链接>，就从一个视频里蒸出这个域的 skill；还有 batch 批量、collect 自动采集守护进程（连接器按配额从 YouTube/GitHub 拉料），skill连同 HF 数据集 microsoft/RESOURCE2SKILL 一起放出。

② skill不是一段文本提示，是"分层多模态 Skill Wiki"：
每个 skill 带结构化文本 + 代码 + 视觉示例 + 元数据 + 来源出处。运行时分两层：
· skills_wiki/ —— 给 agent 浏览、搜索、查看
· skills_library/ —— 域 MCP server 真正执行的可运行资产

视频给"时序操作"、代码给"可执行范式"、文章给"概念地基"，三种模态各补一块。

③ 有跑分，不是纯 demo：
论文里提到：带上蒸馏出的skill，比无技能 agent 提升 11.9 个百分点；在 28 个模型×域格子里赢了 26 个；跨 7 个实操创作域评测。

agent 的"skill"这半年很热，但绝大多数是人手写、再打包分发。Resource2Skill 反过来走自动蒸馏——把散在视频和代码里的高质量做法，熬成技能库，补的是产能的上游一环。

它的"skill"跟 Claude Code 里的 SKILL.md 不是一回事：那个是给 agent 读的 markdown 提示；这里的 skill 是挂在域 MCP server 上、能被真执行的资产，方向是"让 agent 在这些创作工具里干活"，不是"把任意知识写成一段提示词"。

目前绑定创作/办公域：项目落地 5 个（web/ppt/excel/blender/reaper），论文评了 7 个。单条命令最顺的输入还是 YouTube 视频，文章/代码更多走自动采集连接器。

把散落在视频和代码里的高质量方法论，熬成 agent 能直接调用的skill库，这件事以前全靠手写，现在有了可复跑的一整套。

#Resource2Skill #agent技能 #技能蒸馏 #多模态 #MCP #微软

user.name: Sebastian Raschka
user.description:
ML/AI research engineer. Ex stats professor.
Author of "Build a Large Language Model From Scratch" (https://t.co/O8LAAMRzzW) & reasoning (https://t.co/5TueQKx2Fk)

fullText:
Thanks everyone for all the kind words and feedback. Super happy that you are enjoying Build a Reasoning Model (From Scratch)! 

Unfortunately, there's small typo in listing 6.5 on page 198 (see video below).

The line "torch.manual_seed(0)"
should be "torch.manual_seed(5)"

This correction is needed to reproduce the generated response in listing 6.5 and the corresponding log-probability outputs later in Chapter 6. If you use 0, the generated response and the results that follow will be different.

This will be fixed in the next printing. I am sorry about the oversight, and I hope this note saves you some debugging time.

fullText:
Math for Web Design — Precision Layout and Animation: https://t.co/TVdqIKRmka

Amazon summary:

Beautiful websites and apps are rooted in established mathematical principles. Know the math, and you'll gain a superpower when it comes to layout calculations, animating page elements, accurate color rendering, and responsive design. “Math for Web Design” teaches you the math you need to create awesome, precise, and highly functional front-end designs.

You'll learn how to: 

- Improve layouts and animations with the Golden Ratio and proportional scaling.
- Optimize responsiveness and accessibility with percentage-based sizing.
- Create UI effects with physics, vectors, and geometric transformations.
- Master algebra and grid calculations for CSS Grid and Flexbox.
- Grok color theory and blending math.

user.name: AIGCLINK
user.description: 致力于让每个想拥抱AI的人都能找到适合自己的AI产品，助力企业定制AIGC应用

fullText:
29k star的“AI 克隆任意网站"的开源skill来了”，输入/clone-website +<网址>，像素级还原网站：agent开始扒设计 token+素材、写组件规格、在 git worktree 里并行重建、再跟原站做视觉 diff。

但"能扒网站"的工具满地都是，或者直接在claude也能做，这个能冲到 29k star，靠的是它把"怎么让 agent 别糊弄"这件事搞透了：

① 大任务必败，所以它把任务切到很小：
给 agent 一句"把整个 features 区块建出来"，它就会近似间距、猜字号，产出"看着差不多、其实处处不对"。给它一个单独组件 + 精确 CSS 值，它每次都能做对。仓库干脆立了条机械规则：单个 builder 的规格超过 ~150 行，就说明这块太复杂，必须拆——"但它们是一体的"不算理由。

② 规格文件是"合同"，不是备忘：
每个区块先落一份 spec 文件（截图、getComputedStyle() 的真实计算值、下好的本地素材路径、真实文案、多状态），再连同内容内联塞进 builder 的 prompt。没有 spec 就派活，等于让 builder 凭记忆瞎猜。

③ 动手建之前，先判断"这是点击驱动还是滚动驱动"：
仓库把这个叫"最贵的错误"：把一个滚动驱动的区块建成了点击切换的 Tab，就不是调 CSS，是整段重写。所以它的规矩是——先慢慢滚、看东西会不会自己变；不变再去点。

并行工作怎么不冲突且能落地：builder agent 各自在 git worktree 里建自己那块，最后合并、接页面、跟原站做视觉 diff。和"多 agent 隔离干活再汇总"是同一套工程思路。

#clonewebsite #agent克隆网站

fullText:
From WAIC, Kai Fu Li’s https://t.co/HN4oKfrVWX is great biz strategy. 

But kinda weird marketing. 

This is the Palantir of China.

Focused on top-down direct sales for customized AI deployment into regulated industries.

Using their isolated data pools. 

And avoiding the model building frenzy.

That’s good strategy. 

But the marketing is odd. 

They use:

-“money leopards” in the jungle. 

It’s Chinese word play for focusing on monetizing AI asap for clients. 

Smart. But the jungle stuff is odd.

-Kai fu li as celebrity,

Half their waic booth was his books and cut outs to take pictures with.

Overall, An important business to watch.

But very unique.

fullText:
Open-seo： 传统seo工具Semrush/Ahrefs 的开源替代、按量付费、无订阅的平替，还内置 MCP——你可以直接让 Claude Code 帮你查关键词、盯排名、做站点审计，也可 fork 一份 vibe code 出自己的 SEO 工具、还支持GEO。

它想解决的通电是 SEO 工具两个老毛病：贵，和臃肿：

1、无订阅、按量付费：核心数据靠你自带 DataForSEO 的 API key，用多少付多少、直接付给数据方——不用为一堆用不上的功能交年费（这是这个工具里唯一需要为数据付费的地方）；

2、不做大而全的套件，只做聚焦工作流：关键词研究、排名追踪、竞品洞察、外链、站点审计——外加一个很应景的 AI Visibility（你在 AI 答案里的可见性，也就是GEO）。

亮点是支持"agent "调用：

OpenSEO 内置了一个 MCP server，让 Claude Code、OpenClaw、Hermes 这些 agent 直接调用你的 SEO 数据；再配一套 Agent Skills（可复用的 SEO 工作流，指挥 agent 用 MCP 干活）。官方把"best-in-class 的 MCP 和 AI Skills"当第一卖点。也就是说——你可以直接让 Claude Code 帮你查关键词、盯排名、做站点审计，而不是自己在一堆仪表盘里点。你还能 fork 它、vibe code 出自己的定制 SEO 工具。

OpenSEO 把"AI Visibility"做进了工作流，等于顺手接住了 需要GEO 的用户。

#OpenSEO #SEO #开源 #MCP #AI #geo

fullText:
New lecture! This one is a recap of a bunch of history of preferences, the nature of rewards, how RLHF is formulated, which were once seen as central problems in the field. How much as changed. 

Still... super interesting to understand our optimization tools today. Books coming soon :D 

00:00 Intro & context
07:34 A short history of preferences (from Aristotle to the VNM Utility Theorem)
20:17 A brief overview of preference data (from the last two years of my practice)
31:11 Open questions in RLHF data

Lecture 8, covering Chapters 10 & 11 of my book.
